2606.23404.title: ReasoningLens: Hierarchical Visualization and Diagnostic Auditing for Large Reasoning Models
2606.23404.paper_summary: The emergence of Large Reasoning Models has introduced exceptionally long Chain-of-Thought traces, creating a transparency burden where critical logic is often buried under massive procedural text. To address this, we present ReasoningLens, an open-source framework designed for the hierarchical visualization and diagnostic auditing of complex reasoning chains. ReasoningLens addresses information necropsy by: (1) structuring traces into interactive hierarchies that separate high-level strategy from low-level execution; (2) leveraging an agentic auditor for automated error detection and tool-augmented verification; and (3) synthesizing systemic reasoning profiles to reveal model-specific blind spots. By transforming unstructured walls of text into actionable insights, ReasoningLens provides a modular foundation for interpreting, debugging, and optimizing the next generation of reasoning-centric AI.
2606.23404.paper_summary_zh: 大型推理模型的出現引入了異常長的思維鏈痕跡，造成了一種透明度負擔，關鍵邏輯常常埋藏在龐大的程序文本之下。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了ReasoningLens，一個開源框架，旨在對複雜推理鏈進行分層可視化和診斷審計。ReasoningLens通過以下方式解決信息解剖問題：(1) 將痕跡結構化為互動層次，將高層策略與低層執行分開；(2) 利用自主審計工具進行自動錯誤檢測和工具增強驗證；以及(3) 合成系統性推理概況，以揭示模型特定的盲點。通過將非結構化的文本牆轉化為可行的見解，ReasoningLens為解釋、調試和優化下一代以推理為中心的人工智慧提供了一個模組化的基礎。
2606.23404.author: Jun Zhang et.al.
2606.23404.authors: Jun Zhang, Jiasheng Zheng, Boxi Cao, Yaojie Lu, Hongyu Lin, Jia Zheng, Xianpei Han, Le Sun
